family_audit.reason: Round 17 restored the PTX winner surface and recovered runtime from 24.19086361 ms back to 24.17296028 ms. The current source now matches the best PTX surface again, so the restore family is absorbed and should not consume the next implementation round.
The active PTX control family now has one flat-positive result followed by a direct negative wait-window retime, so it remains live in the frontier but no longer deserves the top recommendation.

With the restore family absorbed again, grouped_rows=8 becomes the highest-value alternate PTX family in the repopulated live queue and the cleanest next way to test a different locality regime from the same PTX lineage.
This remains the strongest geometry-side fallback in the frontier and should stay live as the main non-PTX escape hatch.
This preserves another distinct queue family after the search-policy reset and gives the loop a sibling-surface fallback that is neither grouped_rows=8 PTX nor 256x128.
The export-cleanup family remains absorbed on the restored PTX winner surface and should stay filtered out.

directions.name: Restore accepted grouped_rows=8 hot-band consumer ordering
directions.hypothesis: Round 17 restored the PTX winner surface and pulled runtime back from 24.19086361 ms to 24.17296028 ms, with counters returning close to the prior PTX band: active warps rebounded to 16.63%, long-scoreboard tightened to 7.13%, and tensor activity rose to 48.41%. That means the restore family is absorbed again and the loop should stop spending rounds on re-anchoring. The best next move is now the strongest alternate PTX family kept alive from round_history: the grouped_rows=8 consumer-ordering surface. It preserves the PTX hot-band microkernel while materially changing CTA grouping and consumer locality, which makes it the cleanest next attempt to move beyond the current 24.17 plateau without reopening the already mixed control-path family first.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Grouped-row traversal and consumer-order locality on the PTX hot-band microkernel, not the already-restored prologue/refill seam on the grouped_rows=4 winner surface.
directions.risk: Moderate. This is an alternate PTX surface rather than the current default, but it is still the highest-confidence live family once restore is absorbed again.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.172960 ms restored run and the 24.164272 ms best-known run
correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
ranking_notes: This ranks first because restore is absorbed again and grouped_rows=8 is now the highest-value unabsorbed PTX family in the queue.
It preserves the PTX execution style while changing locality enough to be meaningfully different from the grouped_rows=4 winner branch.
It also respects the user's request to keep promising round_history states active in the live queue.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the grouped_rows=8 restore remains materially behind the restored grouped_rows=4 PTX surface without producing a cleaner scheduler signature.

directions.name: Promote The Existing 256x128 Pivot Hot-Band Kernel
directions.hypothesis: The queue still needs one geometry-side branch that is not just another PTX locality retune. The existing 256x128 pivot hot-band kernel remains the strongest such branch in the frontier and keeps a larger reuse tile with its own schedule characteristics. If grouped_rows=8 does not produce enough upside, the next most credible path is to promote the 256x128 pivot kernel and test whether the broader hot-band geometry can amortize control cost better than the 128x128 PTX surface.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Geometry-level latency hiding and control amortization on the 256x128 hot-band path, not the current PTX control-path seam.
directions.risk: Medium to high. This is a broader hot-band dispatch swap onto a non-default geometry, so it carries more surface-change risk than dir_01.
ranking_notes: This stays second because it is still the strongest geometry-side queue family after the PTX restore became absorbed again.
It ranks below dir_01 because grouped_rows=8 is a closer like-for-like alternate from the same PTX lineage.
Keeping it second preserves an orthogonal fallback if the alternate PTX surface also stalls.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the 256x128 promotion cannot get materially closer to the 24.16-24.18 ms PTX band or if it opens a broad hot-band regression without counter improvements.

directions.name: Port grouped-row traversal into the non-PTX 128x128 sibling
directions.hypothesis: The queue should continue to carry at least one sibling-surface family that is neither the PTX grouped_rows=8 branch nor the 256x128 pivot geometry. Porting grouped-row traversal into the non-PTX 128x128 sibling preserves the same broad 128x128 footprint while changing the execution surface away from the PTX microkernel. It stays third because the restored PTX branch is still the measured leader and grouped_rows=8 remains the more natural next alternate, but this sibling path is still worth keeping alive for queue diversity.
directions.expected_bottleneck: CTA traversal and locality on the non-PTX 128x128 sibling surface rather than PTX microkernel control-path behavior.
directions.risk: Medium. This preserves the 128x128 footprint but moves onto a sibling surface that has historically lagged the PTX winner, so upside depends on locality gains outweighing the surface change.
ranking_notes: This keeps one additional distinct family alive after the queue-family contraction.
It ranks below dir_01 and dir_02 because it moves away from the current PTX winner surface without the same level of direct round_history support.
It still deserves the third slot because the user explicitly asked to repopulate and preserve promising queue families.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the non-PTX sibling remains clearly behind the PTX branch without showing a locality or scheduler advantage worth continuing.

notes: Round 18 treats the restore family as absorbed again: the source matches the PTX winner surface and runtime has returned to the 24.17 band. The next recommendation therefore moves to the strongest alternate live family, grouped_rows=8, while 256x128 pivot and the non-PTX 128x128 sibling remain in the diagnosis to preserve queue breadth after the search-policy reset.
